assertions.title: Current docs are reachable from the canonical spine
assertions.reason: The docs spine still leaves some top-level docs outside docs/INDEX without any non-canonical/reference-only labeling, so a reader cannot reliably tell that those surfaces are outside the canonical tree.
evidence: docs/INDEX.md says packets not reachable through the index are outside the canonical docs tree.
docs/ACCOUNTING_PORTAL_SCOPE_GUARDRAIL.md is not indexed and opens with a plain title plus `Status: mandatory, do not remove.` rather than a non-canonical/reference banner.
docs/AUDIT_TRAIL_OWNERSHIP.md is not indexed and opens as a normal doc with `## Canonical Audit Surfaces` rather than a non-canonical/reference banner.
docs/accounting-portal-endpoint-map.md is not indexed and opens as a normal OpenAPI-driven doc without a non-canonical/reference banner.

assertions.title: Superseded docs are deleted or explicitly retired
assertions.reason: The previously stale retained docs sampled in this rerun now carry explicit top-of-file non-canonical/reference banners and point readers to their replacements or to an explicit no-replacement note.
evidence: docs/developer-guide.md is bannered `NON-CANONICAL` and redirects to the indexed module/flow inventories.
docs/endpoint-inventory.md is bannered `REFERENCE ONLY` and redirects readers to `openapi.json` plus indexed canonical docs.
docs/migration-guide.md is bannered `RETIRED / REFERENCE ONLY` and explicitly states there is no maintained canonical replacement for the old CSV/Tally appendix.
Sample legacy frontend/workflow docs now carry matching non-canonical banners and replacement links, and docs/deprecated/INDEX.md registers them.

assertions.title: Retirement registry covers hard cuts
assertions.reason: The retirement registry now covers both sampled hard-cut routes and the retained reference-only docs surfaces, with either a replacement path or an explicit no-replacement statement.
evidence: docs/deprecated/INDEX.md states every entry points to a canonical replacement or explicitly says no replacement exists.
The registry maps `POST /api/v1/catalog/products` to `POST /api/v1/catalog/items` as a hard-cut replacement.
The registry marks legacy raw-material intake endpoints as `No replacement` and explains the surviving GRN path.
The registry now includes the retained legacy handoff/workflow/reference docs that were missing in the earlier failed synthesis.

assertions.title: Canonical naming matches the approved product identity
assertions.reason: The entrypoints use `orchestrator-erp`, but many active canonical docs still describe the current product as `BigBright ERP`, so the naming remediation is incomplete.
evidence: README.md, ARCHITECTURE.md, and docs/INDEX.md now use `orchestrator-erp`.
docs/CONVENTIONS.md, docs/RELIABILITY.md, docs/RECOMMENDATIONS.md, and docs/BACKEND-FEATURE-CATALOG.md still use `BigBright ERP` in active canonical content.
Canonical docs under docs/agents/, docs/adrs/, and docs/modules/ still contain current-product `BigBright ERP` wording.

summary: Re-ran 4 repo-static docs-spine assertions: 2 passed (VAL-DOCS-003, VAL-DOCS-004) and 2 failed (VAL-DOCS-002, VAL-DOCS-007). The earlier migration-guide/retirement-registry gaps are fixed, but unlabeled out-of-spine docs and mixed product naming remain.
